informal_pl: Tożsamość: 1/(1/(1/x + 1) − 1) + 1 = −x. Pojedynczy `field_simp; ring` powinien zamknąć cel; konieczne hipotezy x ≠ 0, 1/x + 1 ≠ 0, 1/(1/x + 1) − 1 ≠ 0 (czyli x ≠ 0, x ≠ −1).
informal_en: Identity: 1/(1/(1/x + 1) − 1) + 1 = −x. A single `field_simp; ring` should close it; the side conditions are x ≠ 0 and x ≠ −1 to keep all denominators alive.
lean_target_signature: theorem successor_negation_identity (x : ℝ) (hx : x ≠ 0) (hx1 : x ≠ -1) : 1 / (1 / (1 / x + 1) - 1) + 1 = -x := by sorry
notes: Pure algebra, no exp/log content. The paper presents this as motivation for the calc-3 −x circuit.
